statements.text: An octopus has three hearts: two pump blood through the gills, and one circulates it to the rest of the body.
Wombats produce cube-shaped feces, formed by varying elasticity along the walls of their intestines.

source.publication: American Physical Society (Physics of Fluids research)

statements.text: Tardigrades have survived direct exposure to the vacuum and radiation of open space during orbital experiments.
Flamingos can only digest their pigment-rich algae diet while standing on one leg, which is why they rarely stand on two.
Giraffes have faintly blue-tinted blood that helps them resist the low-pressure effects of holding their heads so high above their hearts.
